name: Gravity Chute Design Optimization
description: A robotics and manufacturing engineer must determine the optimal tilt angle for a gravity-fed parts chute using physics simulation in CoppeliaSim via the ZMQ Remote API. Model a static chute (2.0x0.5x0.05m) and a dynamic part (0.1x0.1x0.1m) to identify the tilt angle between 10 and 60 degrees that results in an exit velocity closest to 1.5 m/s after 1.8m of travel. Export the simulation results to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/chute_kinematics.csv (columns: angle_deg, descent_time_s, exit_vel_m_s, reached_bottom) and the optimization summary to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/chute_optimization.json (fields: total_angles_tested, target_velocity_m_s, optimal_angle_deg, optimal_velocity_m_s, stuck_count). Login: username=ga, password=password123.
